examples: For all the materialism, trees are still touchingly revered in Japan.
And yet, touchingly, Nadal felt such sympathy for the man whose dream he kept ruining.
Nowhere is Freud more touchingly fallible than in his love letters to his fiancee Martha Bernays, which occupy half this book.
Mcgarrybowen comes across as quaintly, touchingly anachronistic, providing ads from a bygone age that still work.
And once they are used to finding a pleasant room at the inn, customers seem touchingly loyal.
